title: AI Analyst – Product (d/f/m)
description: *Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium\-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time\-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user\-friendly products that delight our 15,000\+ customers and their 1\.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?* **This role requires 2 days per week in the office and is based in Berlin, Dublin or Munich.** We empower Personio’s decision\-making with advanced analytics and strategic insights, while boosting productivity through systems improvement and automation. Be part of our transformative journey in HR technology, where your contributions will shape our data\-driven future. As an **AI Analyst – Product** , you’ll be embedded directly in our cross\-functional product teams, acting as the bridge between product teams, data, and the agents working on top of it. You’ll deliver the instrumentation, impact measurement, and behavioural insights that help our product teams understand their users and ship with confidence. Working closely with senior analysts and engineers, you’ll contribute to the product data context layer that makes our AI\-powered analytics reliable — and ensure data is at the heart of every product decision. **Role Responsibilities: What you'll do** * Instrument \& Track: Own the implementation and maintenance of event\-based tracking for product features, ensuring clean, reliable behavioural data flows into our analytics systems. * Measure Impact: Execute rigorous impact measurement for shipped features — developing testing plans, running experiments, and reporting outcomes with statistical confidence. * Generate Product Insights: Analyse user behaviour through funnels, retention analyses, and segmentation to surface actionable insights that inform squad priorities and backlog decisions. * Build Dashboards \& Data Apps: Create clear, engaging dashboards and lightweight data applications that make product performance visible and self\-serve for product, design, and engineering teams. * Contribute to the Product Context Layer: Help build and maintain the product data \& business context — metric definitions, semantic documentation, and data models — that makes our AI\-powered analytics trustworthy and consistent. * Leverage AI to Accelerate Insight: Use LLMs and AI\-assisted workflows to speed up analysis, automate recurring insight deliverables, and explore new ways to surface signals from product data. * Partner Across Data \& Product: Work closely with Analytics Engineers and Data Scientists to build scalable data models, and with product stakeholders to translate findings into clear, decision\-ready narratives. **Role Requirements: What you need to succeed** * 3\+ years of experience in a data analytics role, with meaningful hands\-on experience supporting product teams in a B2B or B2C product\-led company. * Excellent SQL skills, with experience using dbt for data modelling or a genuine eagerness to develop it quickly. * Proficient in BI and data app tooling, with the ability to build clear, well\-structured dashboards and reports in tools such as Looker, Hex, or similar modern platforms. * Strong foundation in product analytics — comfortable building and interpreting funnels, retention analyses, metric trees, and customer segmentation. * Hands\-on experience with event\-based tracking for web or mobile products, and practical experience with a behavioural analytics tool such as Amplitude, Heap, or Mixpanel. * Demonstrated experience using AI and LLMs in your analytical work — whether through prompt engineering, AI\-assisted insight generation, or building LLM\-powered workflows. * Foundational statistical and programming skills in Python or R, including familiarity with common statistical tests and basic modelling. * Clear communicator: able to translate complex analytical findings into concise, decision\-ready narratives for product, design, and engineering stakeholders. **Why Personio** Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values\-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race. Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work: * Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre\-IPO equity * Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years * Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 Impact Day per year * Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities * We follow an office\-led, remote\-friendly approach, including opportunities to work from home and international locations. Many of our teams have designated days for gathering in the office to enhance collaboration and foster a sense of community. * We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year\-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch.

title: Android Developer - Associate
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** Join our mobile engineering team and help shape the future of Android development at JPMorganChase. You will create impactful, modular applications and frameworks that drive our digital strategy. As a Software Engineer III, you’ll expand your skills, collaborate with talented colleagues, and make a difference every day. Experience a supportive culture where your ideas are valued and your expertise drives innovation. Discover opportunities for growth and advancement in a dynamic environment. As a Software Engineer III in our Mobile Engineering team, you will specialize in Android application and SDK development, building native Android experiences using Kotlin and Java. You will work with us to design modular, reusable Android components and applications that enhance our mobile platforms. You will collaborate with product managers and engineers, participate in architectural decisions, and utilize AI\-assisted development tools. Your contributions will help us deliver secure, scalable, and user\-friendly mobile solutions. **Job responsibilities** * Develop Android applications using Kotlin and Java. * Build and maintain Android SDK and framework components. * Implement Android APIs and platform features. * Write comprehensive unit and instrumentation tests. * Design modular, reusable Android components. * Collaborate with mobile team and product managers. * Participate in architectural decisions for Android applications. * Utilize AI\-assisted development tools. **Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Minimum 4 years of Android development experience. * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience. * Strong foundation in Android development patterns and architecture. * Experience with Android SDK and framework development. * Proficiency in Kotlin and Java programming. * Expertise in Android SDK/framework development and Android APIs. * Experience using GitHub Copilot. **Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Experience designing modular, reusable Android components. * Familiarity with AI\-assisted development tools. * Strong collaboration skills with cross\-functional teams. * Ability to participate in architectural decisions and drive innovation. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our Corporate Technology team relies on smart, driven people like you to develop applications and provide tech support for all our corporate functions across our network. Your efforts will touch lives all over the financial spectrum and across all our divisions: Global Finance, Corporate Treasury, Risk Management, Human Resources, Compliance, Legal, and within the Corporate Administrative Office. You’ll be part of a team specifically built to meet and exceed our evolving technology needs, as well as our technology controls agenda.

title: Apprentice Pipefitter
description: Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly, and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark and Sweden. We recognise that continued business success depends on our commitment to and development of our people. We are determined to create a diverse and equal workforce and to ensure our company appeals to the best and brightest people. We wish for all employees to realise their full potential and have the opportunity to grow and develop during their career with Suir. We are also working continuously to promote a culture of positive wellbeing through our wellbeing programmes and supports to employees and their families through our Employee Assistance Programme and the Lighthouse Club **What you will be doing?** * You will be learning and developing new and practical craft\-related skills, knowledge and competence by: * Interpreting pipefitting drawings and specifications; Planning and sketching piping systems. * Pipefitting, pipe jointing and pipe bending; Inspecting and testing of piping systems and fault diagnosis. * Perform routine maintenance and repairs on piping systems. * Planning and organising the installation of piping systems. **Is this the job for me?** * Minimum O5 in Leaving Certificate Maths. * Up\-to\-date Safe Pass and Manual Handling Training Certifications. * Interest in Mechanical or Electrical systems * Enthusiastic team player with a positive attitude. Good work ethic and solutions focused. * Appreciate the importance of adherence to all safety regulations. Safety First at all times. **In Return From Suir** * 21 days annual leave * SEO Rates, Lodge plus CWPS Pension, Sick pay and Life Assurance contributions * Employee Wellbeing and Employee Assistance Programme * Bike\-to\-Work Scheme * Support to navigate the apprenticeship programme * Opportunity to work overtime * Opportunities for international assignments during apprenticeship to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark or Germany. Lodge €181\.68 if applicable to the project you are working on. **Suir Engineering Apprentice Induction:** Prior to an Apprentice starting on site with Suir Engineering you are required to complete a 4 day induction in our Training Centre in City West, Co. Dublin. **Our way of working** is called the Suir Way, designed to help us continually improve. The benefit for employees is: * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. *At Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer, we value our greatest asset …. our People.*

title: Apprentice Pipefitter (Leixlip)
description: **Company Information and Introduction:** Weltec Engineering is a well\-established and M\&E Engineering Contracting company, delivering high\-quality solutions across major industrial and commercial projects. We are currently expanding our team and are looking for Apprentice Pipefitters at all stages of their apprenticeship to join us on a high\-profile client site in Leixlip. Whether you’re just starting out or already progressing through your apprenticeship, this is an excellent opportunity to build hands\-on experience, learn from skilled tradespeople, and develop a long\-term career in the engineering and construction sector. **Key Responsibilities and Duties:** As an Apprentice Pipefitter, you will work alongside experienced pipefitters and supervisors, gaining practical, on\-the\-job training while developing your technical skills in a real project environment. * Assisting qualified pipefitters with the installation, maintenance, and repair of pipework systems * Learning to read and understand drawings, schematics, and specifications * Using hand and power tools to cut, prepare, and assemble pipes safely and accurately * Following site safety procedures and contributing to a tidy, well\-organised work area * Attending training and continuously developing your skills throughout your apprenticeship **Key Skills and Competencies:*** A genuine interest in a career as a pipefitter or mechanical tradesperson * A positive attitude, strong work ethic, and willingness to learn * Ability to follow instructions and work well as part of a team * Commitment to health and safety on site * Valid Safe Pass (or willingness to obtain) * Manual Handling certification (or willingness to obtain) * Minimum O5 in Leaving Certificate Maths Apprentices at any stage are encouraged to apply

title: Apprentice Sheet Metal Worker
description: *Are you looking to kickstart your career with a growing and dynamic engineering company?* Rockwell are hiring Apprentice Sheet Metal Workers to join our team in Leixlip, Co. Kildare **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Work with sheet steel, galvanised steel, stainless steel and aluminium. * Learn and become proficient in the cutting of these metals by using patterns or templates as guides, as well as shaping the metal by forming, bending, beating or rolling by means of manual and CNC (Computer Numerical Control) machinery. * Collaborate with team members to complete projects efficiently and to the highest standard. * Gain hands\-on experience in working with a variety of mechanical tools and equipment. * Maintain a clean and organised work environment. * Maintain safety standards and adhering to site rules at all times. **Requirements** * Minimum of five passes at leaving certificate level (must include Mathematics and English) * Valid Safe Pass/Manual Handling certificate * No experience necessary but have a keen interest in engineering, mechanical systems * Willingness to commute around the Cork region if required.

title: Architectural Graduate
description: Location: Dublin Employer's Address 26 North Great George’s Street, Dublin 1, D01 X3E5 Closing Date: 14 May 2026 Employer's Website http://www.denisbyrnearchitects.ie Employer's Email info@denisbyrnearchitects.ie Denis Byrne Architects are looking for a talented Architectural Graduate to join the office \- someone who is committed and enthusiastic. Applications only by email, please include a short cover letter, a CV and concise portfolio. Job Requirements: * Relevant qualification in Architecture * Candidates would ideally have previous experience in an architectural practice. * Experience working with CAD is essential and proficiency in Revit an advantage. * Working knowledge of Irish building regulations and statutory processes. * High standard of spoken and written English. * Full eligibility to work in Ireland.

title: Architectural Technician
description: Arcadis is the world's leading company delivering sustainable design, engineering, and consultancy solutions for natural and built assets. We are more than 34,000 people, in over 30 countries, dedicated to improving quality of life. Everyone has an important role to play. With the power of many curious minds, together we can solve the world’s most complex challenges and deliver more impact together. **Role description:** We are seeking a Senior Technical team member to join our Dublin as part of our UK and Ireland Architectural Team, with a focus on a diverse range of projects including Healthcare, Education, Transportation and Mixed\-Use development, both locally and across the UK. This challenge will give you the opportunity to focus on a range of projects for a growing, diverse client base. In this position, you will join a highly creative, collaborative team dedicated to innovative problem solving and design excellence. **Role accountabilities:** * You'll be developing, coordinating and leading the detailed design \& construction phases of projects of varying size, delivering high quality technical solutions, leading elements of complex large and small projects independently and within a team. * Work on all aspects of project delivery \- detailed design development, working drawings, contract administration and on\-site services. * Resolve complex technical and detailed design issues. * Maintain the project documentation, drawings and specifications * Conduct on\-site inspections and document site reviews * Process change control requests during construction and address RFI * Understand and adhere to procedures including quality management and environmental management systems and health \& safety * Promotes the use and continuous development of appropriate systems, procedures and protocols * Supporting the project team with input to programming, resourcing and financial reviews * Work collaboratively with the team and support junior members of staff **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Your sector experience would ideally include recent exposure to health, education and scientific projects in the UK * Ideally MCIAT or regionally equivalent accredited * Experience of working on larger projects as part of a team and day to day leadership for smaller projects * Exemplary Revit ability and experience of complex BIM level 2 projects * Naturally you’ll have a strong knowledge of building regulations, design standards and best practice * Strong organisational skills as well as advanced written / verbal communication skills**:** Why Arcadis? We can only achieve our goals when everyone is empowered to be their best. We believe everyone's contribution matters. It’s why we are pioneering a skills\-based approach, where you can harness your unique experience and expertise to carve your career path and maximize the impact we can make together. You’ll do meaningful work, and no matter what role, you’ll be helping to deliver sustainable solutions for a more prosperous planet. Make your mark, on your career, your colleagues, your clients, your life and the world around you. Together, we can create a lasting legacy. Join Arcadis. Create a Legacy. Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging We want you to be able to bring your best self to work every day which is why we take equality and inclusion seriously and hold ourselves to account for our actions. Our ambition is to be an employer of choice and provide a great place to work for all our people. We believe that by working together diverse people with different experiences develop the most innovative ideas. Equality, diversity and inclusion is at the heart of how we improve quality of life and we work closely with our people across six ED\&I Workstreams: Age, Disability, Faith, Gender, LGBT\+ and Race. A diverse and skilled workforce is essential to our success.

title: Asset & Wealth Management, Business Controls, Associate, Dublin
description: **Job Description** **OUR IMPACT** Across Asset Wealth Management, Goldman Sachs helps empower clients and customers around the world to reach their financial goals. Our advisor\-led wealth management businesses provide financial planning, investment management, banking, and comprehensive advice to a wide range of clients, including ultra\-high net worth and high net worth individuals, as well as family offices, foundations and endowments, and corporations and their employees. Our direct\-to\-consumer business provides digital solutions that help customers save and invest. Across Wealth Management, our growth is driven by a relentless focus on our people, our clients and customers, and leading\-edge technology, data, and design. **YOUR IMPACT** Are you passionate about customer services, operations, leadership, and using digital tools to provide solutions to customers? Do you have a background in risk management and controls operating in the 1st line? We’re looking for a senior risk professional to support our business. **What You’ll Do** * Reporting to the Head of Ireland Business Controls, you will lead our Business Risk and Controls team, ensuring a robust business risk management framework incorporating risk and control self\-assessment and control assurance, business resilience, business continuity and incident management * Responsible for establishing a strong risk cadence across all areas of the Savings business, managing our risks within the required business appetite * Ensures compliance with established policies, procedures and authority levels across multiple cross\-functional teams and processes * Develops strategies to manage Regulatory and Operational Risk, working in collaboration with 2nd and 3rd line teams to identify any risks and managing issues in line with agreed business policies * Provides direction and support to the business leadership team to ensure key controls are in place to manage Regulatory and Operational Risk * Leads a small team of risk professionals, driving the risk strategy along with ensuring all required activities are completed in accordance with the business risk framework * Supports monthly control testing across business procedures, as directed by the leadership team, to identify any gaps in controls of new / emerging risks * Completes ongoing risk assessments; analysing existing risks along with new and emerging risks and identifying, describing and estimating the risks affecting the team and wider business * Works closely with global Risk teams to establish and quantify the risk appetite and ensure this is embedded into policies, procedures and controls * Conducts regular audits of policy and compliance to standards * Partners with Delivery and Training and Development to collect and analyse data from each audit/monitor activity and assist customer support teams with the development and implementation of appropriate corrective action plans, training tips, and team communication to improve overall quality results * Designs appropriate and ongoing risk training materials and facilitates training across all the teams * Completes regular Risk Control Self Assessments for the savings business * Provides monthly Risk and Compliance dashboard * Provides monthly report on control testing * Works with teams, and across the business to ensure the incident management and business continuity plans are well understood **Requirements** * In depth product and regulatory knowledge across retail banking * 5 years of experience in risk management and control testing, ideally operating in the 1st line * In depth understanding of Operational Risk, within a regulated environment * In\-depth knowledge of management, regulatory policies and legal requirements * Degree in Finance or a related field preferred * Excellent business analytical, good eye for detail * Excellent planning or organizational skills * Strong negotiation skills and ability to influence people * Excellent interpersonal, organizational, verbal and written communication skills * Enjoys working in a fast\-paced, changing, start\-up environment * Problem\-solving and decision\-making abilities * Good technical acumen: ability to understand the wider digital business architecture **About Goldman Sachs** At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers. We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability\-statement.html © The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., 2026\. All rights reserved. Goldman Sachs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, veterans status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Assistant Designer - Menswear
description: **Assistant Designer** Because your ideas are our style. Design, our way. As Assistant Designer, you will work closely alongside Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams to translate trend into product. Working alongside a Designer, you will collaborate with fellow product designers across the business you will create strong, fashion forward, commercial and global ranges that identify with the Primark brand. As Assistant Designer, you will demonstrate passion, curiosity and interest in fashion. You will predict key trends using the knowledge of top sellers, extensive research and the knowledge of forecasting tools. You will actively participate in key range building meetings and support product development through the completion of focused technical tasks. You will work within the development process, applying good knowledge of design tools, product qualities and specifications. You will be responsible for staying ahead of trends, continually research new ideas. **What You’ll Do As An Assistant Designer** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Research \& Spot Trends: Identify emerging fashion trends through research and translate them into commercially viable concepts for Primark’s ranges. * Support Range Development: Assist the Designer in building impactful, trend\-led product ranges that balance creativity and commerciality. * Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Designers, Buyers, and Merchandisers to ensure creative vision is executed and aligned with brand strategy. * Manage Design Process: Track design updates, maintain critical path timelines, and prepare trend and design files for presentations and supplier meetings. * Analyse Performance: Review past range performance with Buying and Merchandising teams to inform future design decisions. * Ensure Commercial Awareness: Understand cost implications, competitor activity, and how trends influence sales to support profitable product development. **Key Interactions and Engagements as part of this role:** As Assistant Designer you will report directly to a Designer. You will work closely with Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams, as well as collaborating with Design teams across all product categories to ensure alignment and a unified Design vision across our brand. You will also be expected to represent your product categories on working groups and forums across departmentally **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle. Let’s talk lifestyle: 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * Design Expertise: At least 2 years’ experience in a Trainee or Assistant Designer role, with a solid understanding of fashion design principles. * Relevant Qualification: Degree in fashion design or equivalent practical experience. * Technical Proficiency: Strong skills in Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop to create high\-quality design assets. * Commercial Mindset: Good business acumen and genuine interest in retail, with the ability to balance creativity and commerciality. * Attention to Detail \& Organization: Accuracy, proactive approach, and excellent planning skills to manage deadlines effectively. * Collaborative \& Creative Attitude: Strong communication skills, positive energy, and an innovative mindset to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. Excited? Good. Because it’s energising to put your skills to work, in a growing global business committed to encouraging people to express themselves – and feel a sense of belonging. Apply to be part of Primark’s future, today… and enjoy career growth, our way. **About Primark** Primark is an international clothing retailer employing more than 70,000 colleagues across 16 countries in Europe and the US. Founded in Ireland in 1969 under the Penneys brand, Primark aims to create maximum joy at minimum cost – less money and less impact on the planet. Primark continues to expand across new and existing markets with the aim of reaching 530 stores by the end of 2026\. Every person impacts the culture at Primark. Our values: Caring (we always strive to put people first), Dynamic (we bravely push the boundaries to stay ahead) and Together (we learn more, laugh more and achieve more as a team) outline the behaviours we expect of our people as we work with each other, our customers, our suppliers and our partners. Let’s see what impact you can make! **REQ ID:** JR\-3870

title: Associate Electrical Engineer
description: **Senior Electrical Engineer (Dublin)** **About the Role** An established engineering consultancy is seeking a Senior Electrical Engineer to join its Dublin team. You will lead electrical design delivery across a range of projects (commercial, residential, healthcare, cultural), managing small–medium projects and contributing to larger multidisciplinary schemes. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead electrical design from concept through to completion * Collaborate with clients, project teams, and stakeholders * Ensure high\-quality, compliant engineering solutions * Support client relationships and project cost management * Attend site meetings, inspections, and review technical submissions * Prepare reports and monitor installation quality and progress * Mentor junior engineers and support team development **Requirements** * Degree in Electrical Engineering or Building Services * 5\+ years’ experience in consultancy\-based electrical design * Experience leading projects and supporting junior staff * Chartered status (or working towards it) * Strong communication and problem\-solving skills * Proficiency in tools such as AutoCAD, Revit, Navisworks, Amtech, and DIALux **Working Pattern** * 37\.5\-hour week with flexible hours (8am–6pm, core hours 10am–4pm) **What’s on Offer** * Competitive salary, bonus, and pension * 22–27 days annual leave \+ bank holidays * Structured career development and training support * Health and wellbeing initiatives * Social events and collaborative team culture * Opportunities to work across international projects **Eligibility** Applicants must have permanent residency or unrestricted right to work in Ireland. Visa sponsorship is not available. If you are interested please email a copy of your CV to megan@consciatalent.com for immediate consideration.

title: Associate Professor First Peoples Nursing (Identified role)
description: This Job is based in Australia **Associate Professor First Peoples Nursing (Identified role)** * Employment Type: Full \- time (35 hours a week) * Duration: Continuing * Remuneration: $185k \- $204k base (\+17% superannuation, leave loading) * Work rights: Visa sponsorship is not available for this position. Candidates must hold full rights to work in Australia to be considered for this position. * Location: Some NSW site travel required (Port Macquarie, Liverpool clinical campuses), but flexible working considered. * UNSW offers up to 10 days paid culture leave annually for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander staff members * Opportunity to work within a collaborative community, rather than in isolation *This position is open to Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander applicants only. UNSW has obtained an exemption under section 126 of the Anti\-Discrimination Act 1977 (NSW) to designate and recruit professional and academic positions for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander persons only, to fulfil UNSW’s goal of a representative workforce rate.* **UNSW Discipline of Nursing** The University of New South Wales (UNSW) is establishing a Discipline of Nursing within the School of Population Health. The first nursing program to be developed is the Graduate Entry Master of Nursing Program, that will commence in 2028\. **The Opportunity** This exciting new opportunity is a joint role between Blakcademy and the School of Population Health (SPH), leading and advancing First Peoples Nursing education nationally and internationally. The position provides leadership in curriculum innovation, professional development, student experience, academic compliance, and research. It requires strong expertise in Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander health, including social and cultural determinants and the impacts of colonisation and racism, alongside significant experience in nursing or health education, particularly in designing, teaching, and assessing culturally safe curricula that integrate First Peoples perspectives. **Key Skills And Experience Required** * A PhD in nursing or a related health discipline, and/or relevant tertiary degree, preferably and/or relevant extensive teaching, curriculum design work experience. * Registration with the Nursing \& Midwifery Board of Australia (NMBA) as a registered nurse * Proven commitment to proactively keeping up to date with discipline knowledge and developments. * Demonstrated experience in First Peoples teaching and learning design using a range of pedagogical (including Indigenous) approaches, development and delivery of courses and programs, at undergraduate and/or postgraduate level. * Experience of implementing educational technologies and online delivery methods. * Evidence of teaching effectiveness and passion for First Peoples nursing educational excellence and fostering this in others. * Demonstrated experience with industry or work\-integrated learning relating to First Peoples health. * Evidence of professional development of teaching practice in self and others with the ability to advance and lead an inclusive culture of excellence in First Peoples learning and teaching in nursing or health related programs. * Demonstrated success in initiating First Peoples curriculum development and improvement in nursing or health related programs. * Experience mentoring and supporting early\- and mid\-career academic staff * Evidence of highly developed interpersonal and organisational skills. * Evidence of ability to support and inspire students from diverse backgrounds and support student equity diversity and inclusion initiatives. * Significant track record in research leadership with outcomes of high quality and high international impact with clear evidence of the desire and ability to continually achieve research excellence and deliver research leadership. * Demonstrated leadership in building First Peoples engagement and partnerships with the health profession and industry. *Full details can be found in the position description.* **UNSW Benefits and Culture:** People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world. Our benefits include: * Flexible Working arrangements (work from home, flexible hours) * Additional 3 days leave during Christmas holidays * Cultural duties performed by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander staff members are recognised with reduced workload or additional allowance * Career development opportunities * Up to 50% discount on UNSW courses * Flexible 17% Superannuation contributions, additional leave\-loading payments and salary sacrifice. * Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport) **How To Apply** Please click on Apply now to apply online. Applications should not be sent to the contact listed below. Please provide a resume and a separate cover letter addressing the key skills and experience for the role listed in the Position Description. **Pre\-Employment Checks** **Preferred Required** Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre\-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for. These pre\-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks: * Confirmation of Aboriginality documentation * National and International Criminal history checks * Entitlement to work and ID checks * Working With Children Checks * Completion of a Gender\-Based Violence Prevention Declaration * Verification of relevant qualifications * Verification of relevant professional membership * Employment history and reference checks * Financial responsibility assessments/checks * Medical Checks and Assessments Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW. **Enquiries** * Merita O'Connell – Talent Acquisition Partner E: merita.oconnell@unsw.edu.au * Natalie Chenhall – Manager Indigenous Employment E: n.chenhall@unsw.edu.au **Applications close:** Sunday 17th May before 11\.30pm Sydney time. title: Associate Representative - Inside Sales

Wesco is committed to fostering a workplace where every individual is respected, valued, and empowered to succeed. We promote a culture that is grounded in teamwork and respect. With a workforce of over 20,000 people worldwide, we embrace the unique perspectives each person brings. Through comprehensive benefits and active community engagement, we create an environment where every team member has the opportunity to thrive. Learn more about Working at Wesco here and apply online today! Founded in 1922 and headquartered in Pittsburgh, Wesco is a publicly traded (NYSE: WCC) FORTUNE 500® company. *Wesco International, Inc., including its subsidiaries and affiliates (“Wesco”) provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, religion, color, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics protected by law. US applicants only, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer.* *Los Angeles Unincorporated County Candidates Only: Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act.* *This posting is for a current, active vacancy intended for immediate hire.*

title: Associate Retirement Consultant
description: Posting Description: **Associate Retirement Consultant** Do you have experience in pensions, insurance and/or finance? Are you eager to grow your career? This might be the role for you! We are looking for an Associate Retirement Consultant to work closely with Retirement Consultants as the primary day to day contact for a portfolio of clients, including preparing reports, attending regular client meetings and assisting with the provision of advice to ensure client needs are met. This is a hybrid role with the flexibility to work both virtually and from our Dublin office.#### **Aon is in the business of better decisions** At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world. As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive, diverse team, and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed.#### **Aon \- Wealth Solutions** Aon's Wealth Solutions business is the global leader in human resource consulting and outsourcing solutions. For us, clients are key, and the expertise of our people are second to none. We provide clients with distinctive value through an industry\-leading portfolio of products and services in actuarial, investment, general consulting, and pension administration. We align to a global structure and work to develop methodology, practices, and services to exceed our client’s needs.#### **What the day will look like** * Provide support to consultants, in delivering and managing the clients in their portfolio in accordance with client servicing procedures * Building strong relationships with consultants and clients * Work towards managing your own portfolio of clients * Independently respond to queries and requests from consultants, providers and clients * Stay up to date with industry trends and developments and assist with the identification of revenue generation opportunities for existing clients * Proactively participating in client project work as required * Communicating with clients effectively at all levels, in order to maintain and further develop our excellent client relationships * Attend and input into client meetings as required * You will contribute to the delivery of member presentation materials, and will deliver member presentations as required from time to time * Ensure any appropriative legislative, regulatory, business, practice and professional standards are followed * Analyze information and suggest approaches to work and prepare and review standard reports. * Identify and refer cross selling opportunities for the Actuarial and Investment Practices along with any other cross business opportunities across Aon Ireland * Contribute to the Consultants Priorities Group by assisting and implementing structures which will result in streamlining and creating efficiencies in our client management #### **Skills and experience that will lead to success** * 3\+ years relevant Financial/Professional services experience * Working towards or have achieved QFA or IIPM Professional Qualification, with relevant up to date CPD where required and meeting any relevant regulatory requirements of the role * Experience and successful management of client service delivery * Experience in managing multiple stakeholders and producing quality deliverables on time * Excellent organizational and project management skills, with the ability to manage and plan for multiple projects * Strong IT skills with working knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word \& Power point * You would hold an awareness of our companywide capabilities that can suit our client's needs * High level of self\- motivation and proactivity. * Proactively contributing to the success of the team * Effective problem solving and analytical skills to anticipate future needs/issues, * Ability to effectively plan, prioritize and execute tasks in high pressure environment. * Confidence in managing client queries and expectations * Strong communications, presentation and interpersonal skills with effective client management skills. * Proactively participate in continuous professional development * Experience of pensions is desirable but not essential #### **How this opportunity is different** This is an exciting new opportunity for the As an Associate Retirement Consultant, you will be instrumental in adding value to our relationship with clients and ensuring that we continue to deliver high quality, tailored solutions. **\#LI\-HYBRID** **\#LI\-AO1**

title: Bask Thermal Spa Associate
description: **Job Ref:** OCC3638**Branch:** The Davenport Hotel**Location:** The Davenport, Dublin 2**Salary/Benefits:** Competitive Salary, Free Meal, Birthday Off**Contract type:** Permanent**Hours:** Full Time**Shift pattern:** Varied**Hours per week:** 37\.5**Posted date:** 29/04/2026**Closing date:** 15/05/2026 **About Us:** O'Callaghan Collection is a thoughtfully curated and design led collection of 5 Irish family\-owned and managed luxury boutique hotels located in the heart of Dublin and Gibraltar city centres. Each boutique hotel has its own unique identify that reflects its location and offered guest experience. Our properties are continually being developed in order to exceed evolving customer expectations. Our philosophy centres around tailored guest and client solutions. We prioritise personalised service and experience over a one\-size\-fits\-all approach. We recognise that excellent customer outcomes are directly tied to the satisfaction and engagement of our employees. Therefore, our approach to attracting and retaining talent is centred around creating a workplace culture that values and supports our staff. We offer comprehensive training and development programs to help our team members achieve their career goals and stay up to date with industry trends. In addition to investing in our employees' career development, we also prioritise their well\-being and work\-life balance. We offer competitive compensation packages, flexible scheduling, and a range of employee benefits and perks. Our goal is to attract and retain top talent in the industry who share our commitment to excellence and passion for providing exceptional service to our guests. If this sounds like you, we’d love to connect. We are Caring\|Proud\|Accountable\|Determined. **Benefits:** * Subsidized membership at our fully equipped gym * Your Birthday as an extra paid day off! * Enhanced holidays with service * Rewards programme * Refer a friend \- up to €1000 reward * Discounted room stays \- friends and family * Discounted FB when you are a guest in any of our hotels * The opportunity to progress \- 4 Hotels in Dublin city and a Hotel in Gibraltar * Tax saver for travel tickets * Bike to work scheme and a safe place to leave your bike **Role information:** As a **SPA Associate**, you will help create a serene, welcoming, and impeccably maintained spa environment. Your role focuses on delivering exceptional guest service while ensuring smooth daily operations. You will: * Welcome guests warmly and manage check\-ins/outs with professionalism. * Explain spa facilities, etiquette, and policies clearly. * Handle locker allocation, guest belongings, and spa flow with care and accuracy. * Maintain high standards of cleanliness in all spa areas, including wet zones. * Support bookings, modifications, and payments using Opera Trybe systems. * Anticipate and respond to guest needs with discretion, sensitivity, and confidentiality. * Ensure safety, hygiene, and brand standards are upheld at all times. * Communicate guest preferences, requests, or concerns to management. * Assist with inventory control, towel/robe management, and daily checklists. * Work collaboratively with the Spa, Reception, and Housekeeping teams. * Handle guest complaints calmly and professionally. * Identify upselling opportunities when appropriate to enhance guest experience. **Requirements:** * Previous experience in hospitality, spa, wellness, or luxury guest service. * A qualification in Spa, Wellness, or Hospitality (preferred). * Excellent spoken and written English communication skills. * Strong organization and multitasking abilities. * A naturally calm, polished, and professional personal presentation. * Exceptional guest care skills with high levels of discretion and empathy. * Knowledge of spa etiquette, wellness services, and basic safety standards. * Competence using computer systems (Opera experience is a plus). * The ability to work both independently and as part of a supportive team. * A proactive, solution\-focused mindset, especially during busy periods. Located in the heart of Georgian Dublin, The Davenport is one of Dublin’s most iconic hotels that blends seamlessly its heritage and history with the modern needs of today’s hotel guest. Elegant décor and a choice of spaces make The Davenport an ideal choice for a special occasion or important work event. Our restaurant and intimate bar offers delicious food and drinks in beautiful and comfortable surroundings, including a stunning traditional afternoon tea with a contemporary twist. The needs of the modern traveller have not been forgotten – comfortable bedrooms feature smart TVs with Chromecast and Dublin’s fastest hotel WiFi (1GB), while every guest also has access to a 24\-hour fully equipped gym. We are consistently ranking high on TripAdvisor for our exceptional service and unforgettable guest experiences!

title: Business and Techincal Consultant
description: **Introduction** A career in IBM Consulting is built on long\-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long\-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences. **Your Role And Responsibilities** We are seeking a Business and Application Consultant. This role is vital to the continued success of IBM’s delivery for one of our largest and strategically important clients. Ideal candidate will have a mix of solution and integration architecture experience within SAP SuccessFactors. The position requires a consultant who can lead and execute solution design and process adoption activities across a complex, multi\-stakeholder environment, supporting the client as they continue to modernise, scale, and transform their HR domain, with knowledge and experience of COE processes, Change management, business analysis **Key Additional Responsibilities** Client Engagement \& Relationship Management * Maintain strong working relationships across junior, mid\-level, and senior client stakeholders. * Act as a trusted advisor, supporting client decision\-making and ensuring alignment with IBM methodologies and programme outcomes. * Represent IBM professionally and positively in all client interactions. Team Leadership \& Mentoring * Mentor and support junior IBM team members, acting as a catalyst for capability uplift across the Business Change and Training team. * Foster a collaborative environment that improves performance, reduces dependence on higher\-band resources, and supports delivery excellence. Programme Delivery * Collaborate closely with the broader IBM delivery team to ensure integrated and timely delivery across all programme workstreams. * Identify risks, dependencies, and issues early, and support the creation of mitigation strategies. * Contribute to quality assurance, continuous improvement, and knowledge management activities. Future Growth \& Opportunity Development * Support the account team in identifying and shaping future work across similar clients. * Contribute to proposals, client presentations, and thought leadership to demonstrate IBM’s value and expand our footprint. **Preferred Education** Bachelor's Degree **Required Technical And Professional Expertise** * Process Mapping \& Business Analysis: Ability to interpret business processes, conduct impact analyses, create process maps, and translate system changes into training and communication requirements. * Enterprise Application Familiarity: Experience working with large\-scale enterprise platforms (e.g., MS Dynamics, SAP, Salesforce, custom national health systems), including understanding how system changes affect business workflows. * Stakeholder Analysis \& Reporting Tools: Proficient in using tools such as Excel, Power BI, Confluence, or Jira to support stakeholder mapping, readiness assessments, progress reporting, and project documentation. * Project Delivery Tools \& Governance: Competence with project delivery environments such as MS Project, Jira, SharePoint, Teams, and IBM delivery governance processes to ensure structured and compliant execution. * Exposure to Service\-Oriented Architecture (SOA): Familiarity with designing and implementing SOA principles to break down business applications into individual functions and processes, enabling them to run independently of applications and computing platforms. * Experience with Web Services: Knowledge of building and deploying Web services using standards and specifications, such as protocols to describe operations and data exchange, to enable communication between independent applications. * Understanding of Enterprise Integration: Exposure to integrating Web services to define a particular Web service application in SOA and Enterprise Integration, using protocols to describe operations to execute or data to exchange with other Web Services. * Familiarity with Software Integration: Experience working with software that enables communication between independent applications, utilizing a collection of standards and specifications to build and deploy new functions and processes. * Exposure to Interaction Protocols: Understanding of implementing interaction between Web services using protocols to enable them to communicate with each other and form a cohesive Web service application. * Advanced Web Services Knowledge: Experience with building and deploying Web services using standards and specifications, such as protocols to describe operations and data exchange, to enable communication between independent applications. Familiarity with designing and implementing Web services to define a particular Web service application in SOA and Enterprise Integration. * Enterprise Integration Expertise: Exposure to integrating Web services to define a particular Web service application in SOA and Enterprise Integration, using protocols to describe operations to execute or data to exchange with other Web Services. Understanding of implementing interaction between Web services using protocols to enable them to communicate with each other. * Software Integration Skills: Experience working with software that enables communication between independent applications, utilizing a collection of standards and specifications to build and deploy new functions and processes. Familiarity with designing and implementing software integration solutions to enable independent applications to communicate with each other. * Design Business Functions: Design individual business functions and processes from everyday business applications, allowing them to run independently of the applications and computing platforms on which they operate. * Build and Deploy: Build and deploy new functions and processes using software that enables communication between independent applications, utilizing a collection of standards and specifications. * Integrate Web Services: Integrate Web services to define a particular Web service application in SOA and Enterprise Integration, using protocols to describe operations to execute or data to exchange with other Web Services. * Implement Interaction: Implement interaction between Web services to enable them to communicate with each other and form a cohesive Web service application. **Preferred Technical And Professional Experience** * Cloud\-Native Architecture Exposure: Exposure to designing and implementing cloud\-native architectures across multiple cloud platforms, including containerization, microservices, and API\-driven development. * Modernization Methodology Familiarity: Exposure to application modernization methodologies such as AMM and tools like ICA to accelerate transformation. * Generative AI Integration Knowledge: Exposure to integrating generative AI capabilities into modernized applications, including various AI technologies.

title: CMEV & MVHR Service Technician
description: **CMEV \& MVHR Service Technician** **Pay: From €50,000\.00 depending on experience** **Job Description \| CMEV \& MVHR Service Technician** **Location: Office in Meath / Leinster Region (site\-based)** **Employment Type: Full\-time / Permanent** We are currently seeking an experienced **CMEV \& MVHR Service Technician** to join our growing team. This role involves servicing, maintaining, fault\-finding, and commissioning mechanical ventilation systems in domestic and light commercial properties across Ireland. **Duties and Responsibilities** Carry out servicing, maintenance, and inspection of CMEV (Central Mechanical Extract Ventilation) and MVHR (Mechanical Ventilation with Heat Recovery) systems in domestic and light commercial properties. Perform planned preventative maintenance in line with manufacturer recommendations, industry standards, and company procedures. Conduct system fault‑finding and diagnostics, identifying performance issues and carrying out corrective works where required. Clean, adjust, and replace system components, including filters, fans, ducts, grilles, valves, and controls, to ensure optimal performance. Commission CMEV and MVHR systems following service or installation, including: o Airflow measurement and balancing o System performance checks o Verification of controls and settings o Complete and issue all required service reports, commissioning sheets, and maintenance documentation accurately and on time. o Ensure all works comply with manufacturer guidelines, building regulations, and health \& safety requirements. Liaise professionally with customers, explaining: o System operation o Maintenance requirements o Any identified faults or recommended remedial works Maintain a high standard of workmanship with a strong focus on quality, accuracy, and attention to detail. Work efficiently in a busy, job‑based environment, managing multiple service visits and adhering to scheduled appointments. Use hand tools, power tools, airflow testing equipment, and diagnostic instruments safely and competently. Demonstrate a willingness to learn and upskill, keeping up to date with new ventilation products, controls, and best practices. Display a high level of enthusiasm, reliability, and self‑motivation, with excellent timekeeping and attendance. Work collaboratively with installation teams, office administration, and management when required. Carry out other duties as necessary, once appropriate training and competency has been achieved. **Skills and Qualifications** Experience servicing and maintaining CMEV and/or MVHR systems (or strong mechanical background with willingness to be trained) Diploma or Degree (Level 6 or higher) in Construction or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. Full Irish Driving Licence. Excellent attention to detail with a strong commitment to delivering high‑quality work. Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with the ability to identify and resolve issues efficiently on site. Well‑developed interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with customers, colleagues, and site personnel. Strong organisational skills, with the ability to manage workload and priorities effectively. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Good knowledge of building construction materials and building services, including airtightness and ventilation systems (or willingness to develop this knowledge). Flexible and motivated, with a strong drive to complete work assignments within committed deadlines. Ability to manage a high volume of work accurately, even in a fast‑paced environment. What We Offer * Competitive rates of pay (Starts at €50,000 based on experience) * Consistent, long\-term work * Training and upskilling opportunities in airtightness systems * Supportive team environment * Opportunity to work on modern, energy\-efficient building projects * Company Pension * Annual Team Building Events **How to Apply** **To apply, please send your CV and Cover Letter to info@scenergyrating.ie** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Company events * Company pension * On\-site parking * Statutory Sick pay Experience: * Servicing, maintenance, and inspection of CMEV (Central Mechanical Extract Ventilation) and MVHR (Mechanical Ventilation with Heat Recovery) systems: 1 year (required) · Work Location: In person \& Site based Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company car * Company events * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay Ability to commute/relocate: * Navan, County Meath: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * • Why do you think you are a good fit for our Company? (Note: Only reasonable replies reviewed) * A cover letter is required. Please include an example of your experience in MVHR \& CMEV maintenance and servicing. Education: * Advanced/Higher Certificate (required) Experience: * Servicing, maintenance, and inspection of CMEV\&MVHR system: 1 year (required) Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * Safepass Card (required) * Manual Handling Certificate (required) * A Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Willingness to travel: * 100% (required) Work Location: In person

title: Case Official – Credit Collections & Support Team – 6 Month Fixed Term Contract
description: At IVC Evidensia, we are a leading veterinary services provider in Europe and North America with more than 2,500 clinics and hospitals across 19 countries and around 42,000 employees. We aim to have an extraordinary impact on animal lives, supporting in the region of 25,000 animals every single day across our international network. IVC Evidensia exists to champion the long\-term health and welfare of animals and deliver our purpose of Healthy Animals, Happy Owners. At IVC Evidensia, our Central Collections team plays a vital role in supporting our veterinary practices and clients by managing outstanding balances in a fair, professional, and customer\-focused way. As a Case Official, you will be responsible for managing client accounts, engaging with clients through inbound and outbound contact, agreeing appropriate repayment solutions, and resolving cases efficiently while maintaining high standards of service, compliance, and empathy. This is an entry\-level role and a great opportunity for someone looking to build experience in a structured, supportive collections or customer contact environment, with clear development pathways into senior and leadership roles. **This role is being offered on a 6\-month fixed term contract basis.** **Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities** **Client \& Case Management** * Manage client accounts through inbound and outbound communication channels (phone, email, letters). * Engage with clients to understand their circumstances and agree appropriate repayment solutions. * Resolve assigned cases through standard processes, ensuring timely and accurate outcomes. * Maintain a professional, empathetic, and customer\-focused approach in all interactions. **Operational Delivery** * Work to agreed treatment strategies, policies, and processes to deliver consistent outcomes. * Meet individual productivity, quality, and compliance targets. * Maintain accurate and complete case notes, records, and documentation. * Escalate complex, sensitive, or vulnerable cases appropriately to Senior Case Officials or Team Leaders. **Compliance, Risk \& Quality** * Adhere to Debt Management Policy, Client Vulnerability Policy, Data\-Protection Policy and standards, and complaints procedures as well as our IVC Code of Responsibility. * Identify and flag potential vulnerability indicators and follow agreed support processes. * Participate in quality assurance activities and respond positively to feedback and coaching. * Maintain auditable records and follow required controls and procedures. **Team \& Continuous Improvement** * Participate in training, coaching, and team development activities. * Contribute to a positive team culture focused on accountability, learning, and continuous improvement. * Share feedback and ideas to improve client experience and operational effectiveness. **Experience/Qualifications** **Essential:** * Experience in a customer service, contact\-centre, collections, or client\-facing role. * Confident communicator with the ability to handle sensitive or challenging conversations calmly and professionally. * Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow processes and maintain accurate records. * Basic numeracy and literacy skills, with confidence using systems and technology. * Willingness to learn, develop, and take on feedback. **Desirable** * Previous experience in collections * Experience working in a regulated or client\-sensitive environment (e.g. healthcare, financial services, utilities). * Awareness of vulnerability or affordability considerations (training will be provided). title: Change Analyst
description: **Unijobs** on behalf of our public sector client has a requirement for a **Change Analyst.** This is a **temporary** position; the initial contract duration is for **12 months** with the possibility of extension thereafter. This position is also working off a **hybrid model,** and the base location is in the **Leinster area** . Based off a **35\-hour work week** the successful candidate will be employed as an agency employee and will be paid an **hourly rate of €28\.60** based off an **annualised salary of €52,235** and will **accrue 30 days’ annual leave** per year along with **public holidays** . **\*Applicants must be eligible to work in Ireland at the time of application\*** **Person specific requirements:** * Service Management experience, particularly in the area of Service Transition/Change Management/Release Management. * Ideally ITIL Foundation certified or commit to work towards certification * Broad familiarity and experience with modern enterprise architecture from either a technical or service process perspective * Experience with Office 365, SharePoint and Power BI. **Duties and responsibilities:** * Receive, log, and allocate a priority to change tickets * Review logged normal and emergency change tickets and follow up with requestors and other change stakeholders to get change tickets ready for CAB approval * Monitor standard change tickets and provide guidance to change requestors * Collate and document all relevant data that support the Change Management Process and the knowledge management requirement for eHealth support and the business * Is an advocate for the Change Management function * Provide support in carrying out ad\-hoc Change Management tasks * be subject to Garda Vetting and Foreign Police Clearance, if applicable you will be required to obtain these prior to commencing in this role\* **Unijobs is an equal opportunities employer**

title: Chef

present food to the highest standard in line with company guidelines and client specifications. * Ensure all meal service deadlines are consistently achieved. * Take full responsibility for kitchen operations during evening and weekend shifts. * Monitor portion control and presentation standards to ensure consistency. * Ensure all dishes meet quality expectations before leaving the kitchen. * Support delivery of hospitality and special event catering as required. * Maintain allergen awareness and ensure accurate food labelling and communication. * Provide clear instruction, guidance, and support to junior team members. * Assist the Head Chef in the day\-to\-day management of the kitchen. * Step into leadership responsibilities in the absence of senior chefs. * Promote teamwork, food concepts. * Assist in menu planning, costing, and pricing strategies. * Adhere strictly to all food hygiene legislation and company policies. * Ensure full compliance with HACCP and food safety management systems. * Maintain high standards of cleanliness and organisation within the kitchen. * **What you'll bring:** * Proven experience as a Chef de Partie or similar role. * Strong culinary skills and knowledge of modern cooking techniques. * Experience in high\-volume catering or contract catering environments desirable. * Knowledge of HACCP and food safety regulations. * Strong leadership and communication skills. * Ability to work under pressure and meet strict deadlines. * Good financial awareness and understanding of food cost control. * QQI Level 6 * **Why Sodexo?:**
